title: Measurement Benchmarks
essential_question: How do we pick the right unit to measure?

learn.title: Customary Length
learn.content: inch (in), foot (ft), yard (yd), mile (mi). Pencil ≈ 7 in; door ≈ 7 ft; football field = 100 yd; 15 min walk ≈ 1 mi.
learn.tts: inch (in), foot (ft), yard (yd), mile (mi). Pencil ≈ 7 in; door ≈ 7 ft; football field = 100 yd; 15 min walk ≈ 1 mi.

learn.title: Customary Weight
learn.content: ounce (oz), pound (lb), ton (T). Slice of bread ≈ 1 oz; loaf ≈ 1 lb; small car ≈ 1 T.
learn.tts: ounce (oz), pound (lb), ton (T). Slice of bread ≈ 1 oz; loaf ≈ 1 lb; small car ≈ 1 T.

learn.title: Customary Capacity
learn.content: cup (c), pint (pt), quart (qt), gallon (gal). Milk glass ≈ 1 c; milk carton ≈ 1 gal.
learn.tts: cup (c), pint (pt), quart (qt), gallon (gal). Milk glass ≈ 1 c; milk carton ≈ 1 gal.

learn.title: Metric Length
learn.content: millimeter (mm), centimeter (cm), meter (m), kilometer (km). Fingernail ≈ 1 cm; doorway ≈ 2 m.
learn.tts: millimeter (mm), centimeter (cm), meter (m), kilometer (km). Fingernail ≈ 1 cm; doorway ≈ 2 m.

learn.title: Metric Mass
learn.content: gram (g), kilogram (kg). Paperclip ≈ 1 g; textbook ≈ 1 kg.
learn.tts: gram (g), kilogram (kg). Paperclip ≈ 1 g; textbook ≈ 1 kg.

try.question: Best unit for a bed length: inch, foot, or mile?
hints: Think about benchmarks.
Use the conversion factor.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is foot.

try.question: Best unit for a watermelon weight: ounce, pound, or ton?
hints: Think about benchmarks.
Use the conversion factor.
solution_steps: Heavier than an ounce, much less than a ton.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is pound.

try.question: Best unit for water in a swimming pool: cup or gallon?
hints: Think about benchmarks.
Use the conversion factor.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is gallon.

try.question: Best metric unit for a walking trail distance: cm, m, or km?
hints: Think about benchmarks.
Use the conversion factor.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is km.

try.question: Best unit for a paperclip mass: g or kg?
hints: Think about benchmarks.
Use the conversion factor.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is g.
